reasoning: The document exhibits catastrophic content loss, evidenced by the explicit omission of 9 major policy sections and approximately 14 pages of text, replaced by placeholders. This is further compounded by severe structural failures, including a 188% heading count mismatch and the incorrect injection of metadata into the signature block, making the document legally and technically unfit for publication.

concerns.analysis: A massive portion of the document is missing. Entire pages (approximately pages 2 through 10, and 13 through 17) and major policy sections (RA-1 Policy and Procedures, RA-2 Security Categorization, RA-3 Risk Assessment, RA-4/RA-5 Vulnerability Monitoring and Scanning) are omitted. These sections represent the core regulatory requirements of the policy. The output explicitly contains a placeholder field indicating that 9 sections were omitted.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The source PDF contains core technical and procedural sections: RA-1 (pages 2-3), RA-2 (page 6), RA-3 (pages 7-10), and RA-5 (pages 13-17) along with various supporting analysis activities and procedural descriptions.

concerns.analysis: The output exhibits significant content duplication at the end of Section 16 (APPENDIX A). The signature block ('Printed Name of Secretary/CIO or Designee' and 'Signature of Secretary/CIO or Designee') appears twice. This is not a repetition in the source document, which only has one signature block on the final page (page 21).
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The signature block appears only once on page 21 of the PDF.
concerns.output_quote: The signature block text ('Printed Name of Secretary/CIO or Designee' and 'Signature of Secretary/CIO or Designee') appears as elements 22-23 and is repeated as elements 26-27 in the last section.

concerns.analysis: The reading order is materially disrupted at the end of the document. In Section 16, header metadata (the 'Document Information' table and the State Seal image) is incorrectly injected into the middle of the signature block. In the source, these elements are part of the page header, while the signatures are the concluding elements of the text flow.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The State Seal and 'Document Information' block are headers at the top of page 21. The signature lines are at the bottom.
concerns.output_quote: The signature block starts (elements 22-23), followed by the Document Information table (element 24) and the Seal image (element 25), before repeating the signature block (elements 26-27).

concerns.description: Heading count mismatch between JSON and HTML
concerns.analysis: There is a significant mismatch between the number of headings defined in the semantic JSON (9) and those rendered in the final HTML (26). This difference (188%) far exceeds the 10% tolerance threshold, indicating that the renderer is likely incorrectly promoting non-heading elements or section titles to headings.

The signature block intended for the Secretary/CIO or Designee from Page 21 is visually duplicated at the end of the HTML document. One instance appears before the final 'Document Information' metadata block (including visual underscore characters from the original line), and a second instance of the text appears again after the metadata block.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Printed Name of Secretary/CIO or Designee [Date] ... Signature of Secretary/CIO or Designee
concerns.output_quote: The text 'Printed Name of Secretary/CIO or Designee' and 'Signature of Secretary/CIO or Designee' appears twice at the end of the document, separated by the final metadata table.
concerns.location: End of document, following content from page 21

concerns.description: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
concerns.analysis: There is a significant OCR-related difference in the definition of 'Senior Management' where 'ESRMO' (Enterprise Security and Risk Management Office) has been converted to 'SCRO'. SCRO is not a defined term in this policy and this change alters the meaning of the reporting obligation.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: ...reporting to the ESRMO what mitigation actions have been taken.
concerns.output_quote: ...reporting to the SCRO what mitigation actions have been taken.
concerns.location: Section: Responsibilities, Table: IT Risk Assessment Responsibilities by Role, Row: Senior Management

action_items: Re-process the original PDF to ensure all pages are captured; investigate why the pipeline generated placeholders for core content.
Fix reading order logic to prevent header/footer metadata from interrupting signature flows.
Correct OCR dictionary to recognize state-specific acronyms like ESRMO.
